name: PE-22-28
subtitle: TREK-1 Channel Blocker | Shortened Spadin Analog
overview: Synthetic heptapeptide derived from Spadin positions 22-28, functioning as potent TREK-1 antagonist with enhanced selectivity and duration versus parent compound. Primary research focus on rapid antidepressant effects.
mechanism: Selectively blocks TREK-1 potassium channels (IC50: 0.12 nM). Enhances serotonin neurotransmission in dorsal raphe nucleus, triggering CREB activation and hippocampal neurogenesis.

items.name: Depression
items.description: Primary research focus with rapid effects in behavioral models within 4 days.

items.name: Anxiety
items.description: Anxiolytic properties demonstrated in preclinical anxiety models.

items.name: Hippocampal Neurogenesis
items.description: Nearly doubles BrdU-positive cells after 4-day treatment.

items.name: Synaptogenesis
items.description: Promotes new synapse formation through CREB activation.

items.name: Memory Support
items.description: Hippocampal and prefrontal cortex TREK-1 expression supports memory.

items.name: Neuroprotection
items.description: Potential ischemic protection and neuronal survival support.

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous injection to abdominal fat or thigh with site rotation.

steps: Inject BAC water slowly down vial wall

deliveryMethods.overview: Limited data from animal studies only; higher doses required than injection.
Theoretical route; unstudied for PE-22-28 specifically.

interactions.notes: Both enhance serotonergic pathways; monitor for excessive activity.
Different mechanisms for anxiety and mood support.

timeline.effects: Measurable antidepressant effects in preclinical models
Potential mood and cognitive improvements emerge
Sustained treatment allows full neurogenic effects

common: No effects on TREK-2, TRAAK, TASK-1 channels observed
No cardiac dysfunction or seizures in preclinical studies
stopSigns: Severe mood changes or suicidal ideation
monitoring: Monitor for serotonin syndrome if combined with serotonergic medications

bad: Clumped or sticky powder indicating moisture damage

references.title: Shortened Spadin Analogs Display Better TREK-1 Inhibition
references.keyFindings: PE-22-28 IC50 0.12 nM vs 40-60 nM for Spadin; ~23 hour duration. Frontiers in Pharmacology.

references.title: Spadin: A Sortilin-Derived Peptide Targeting TREK-1 Channels
references.keyFindings: Original discovery of TREK-1 blockade as antidepressant mechanism. PLOS Biology.

references.title: TREK-1 Deletion Results in Depression-Resistant Phenotype
references.keyFindings: TREK-1 knockout mice show depression-resistant behavior in 5 tests. Nature Neuroscience.
